mission.title: Install and Configure Spiffe on Kubernetes
mission.description: The SPIFFE Project provides a framework for securely identifying and communicating between application services. Installing SPIFFE allows you to implement a robust identity management system for your microservices architecture.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the SPIFFE Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add spiffe https://spiffe.github.io/helm-charts-hardened
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install the SPIFFE Helm chart
steps.description:
Install the SPIFFE step SSH chart in a new namespace called 'spiffe'.
```bash
helm install spiffe spiffe/spiffe-step-ssh --namespace spiffe --create-namespace --version 0.1.1
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the SPIFFE pods are running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace spiffe
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Configure resource limits for the SPIFFE deployment.
```bash
kubectl set resources deployment spiffe --namespace spiffe --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi --requests=cpu=250m,memory=256Mi
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the SPIFFE Helm release from the cluster.
```bash
helm uninstall spiffe --namespace spiffe
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) associated with SPIFFE.
```bash
kubectl delete crd <crd-name>
```

uninstall.title: Remove the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'spiffe' namespace to clean up all resources.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace spiffe
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current configuration and resources.
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace spiffe -o yaml > spiffe-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure your Helm repository is up to date with the latest charts.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade the SPIFFE Helm chart
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the SPIFFE installation to the latest version.
```bash
helm upgrade spiffe spiffe/spiffe-step-ssh --namespace spiffe --version 0.1.1
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the SPIFFE pods are running the new version successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace spiffe
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
If your SPIFFE pods are stuck in Pending, check for resource availability.
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace spiffe
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods crashing with error
troubleshooting.description:
If your SPIFFE pods are crashing, check the logs for errors.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace spiffe
```

troubleshooting.title: Helm release not found
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter a 'release not found' error, ensure that the release name and namespace are correct.
```bash
helm list --namespace spiffe
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of SPIFFE will have all pods running in the 'spiffe' namespace without errors. You can verify this by checking the pod status and ensuring they are in the 'Running' state.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add spiffe https://spiffe.github.io/helm-charts-hardened
helm repo update
kubectl set resources deployment spiffe --namespace spiffe --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi --requests=cpu=250m,memory=256Mi

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and Helm installed on your local machine.
